subject: Public Health
title: The HIV/AIDS Crisis
description: A 60-second lesson on The HIV/AIDS Crisis.

hook.text: In 1981, doctors noticed rare cancers in young gay men. No one yet knew a new virus had arrived.

buildup.text: HIV crossed from chimpanzees to humans in central Africa decades before anyone noticed.

discovery.text: The virus destroys immune cells slowly—patients felt fine for years while spreading it unknowingly.

twist.text: Stigma killed alongside the virus—governments ignored AIDS because it first hit marginalized communities.

climax.text: Antiretroviral therapy now lets people with HIV live normal lifespans—but 40 million have died so far.

punchline.text: Stigma delayed the response. Science eventually caught up.

quiz.question: How many people have died from HIV/AIDS globally?
